commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c02abba1aa1d03cd0cbcc770368b32b9fcf4ed25)
commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves `browser`, `cron`, and `message` through pre-catalog lean filtering, keeps final model-visible filtering, adds focused Vitest coverage, and updates local-model docs.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +52, Docs 0. Total +70 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main filters the lean-denied tools before Tool Search / Code Mode can catalog them. I did not run a live local-model reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer should resolve the compatibility semantics and config-help alignment before merge; this is not a standalone cleanup close or replacement-branch candidate.

target.title: fix(agents): keep lean tools behind catalog controls
target.body:
Summary:
- Keep `localModelLean` from removing heavyweight tools before Code Mode or Tool Search can place them behind compact catalog controls.
- Preserve the final visible lean-mode filter, so `browser`, `cron`, and `message` stay out of the model-visible schema when lean mode is on.
- Add focused coverage for pre-catalog vs final visible filtering and update local-model docs.

Verification:
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/experimental-features.md docs/gateway/local-models.md docs/providers/ollama.md`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --shell -- "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean-catalog.test.ts"`

Behavior addressed: lean local-model runs no longer lose policy-allowed `browser`, `cron`, and `message` capability before compact catalog controls can represent those tools cheaply.
Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux runner, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_7f1ddde321a8`, slug `quick-krill`, run `run_84ace5cd72c8`, branch rebased onto `origin/main` at `850f7c24d4`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: checked the rebased diff, checked docs formatting for the touched docs, and ran the focused `local-model-lean-catalog` Vitest file remotely through Crabbox.
Evidence after fix: diff check passed; docs formatter reported `Docs formatting clean (647 files)`; Crabbox Vitest reported 1 file and 3 tests passed.
Observed result after fix: lean mode keeps heavyweight tools available to compact catalog controls before the final model-visible filtering step, while the final visible schema still excludes the heavyweight tools for local lean models.
What was not tested: full repo check. Testbox-through-Crabbox is unavailable in this environment because Blacksmith auth is missing, so focused remote proof used the repo AWS Crabbox provider.

Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
